title: ig-central — Audit Event Envelope
description: Common envelope for every audit event written by every container. Single contract for cross-tier correlation and chain integrity. Per `version-2-architecture.md` §7.4 and CSV #4 / #6 / #7. Used by `audit.tier1.{eventType}` (DOMAIN) and `audit.tier2.{eventType}` (SYSTEM) channels declared in `contracts/audit/asyncapi.yaml`.

eventId.description: ULID — time-sortable unique id (Crockford's base32, 26 chars). Sortability matters: Tier 2 stores often page by id and rely on lexicographic order matching chronological order.

eventType.description: Event family identifier (uppercase snake-case). Stable across services. See `version-2-architecture.md` §7.6 for the canonical list.

tier.description: `DOMAIN` = Tier 1 (compliance / audit-of-record). `SYSTEM` = Tier 2 (operations / debugging). Tier 3 (distributed traces) is published via OpenTelemetry, not via this envelope — `traceparent` here references back to those spans.

schemaVersion.description: Envelope schema version. Bumped on breaking changes; old events remain readable for the full retention window.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp. Generated by the producing service, not by the collector — the collector preserves the producer's timestamp.

documentId.description: Convenience correlation key for document-anchored events. Mirrors `resource.id` when `resource.type=DOCUMENT`. Allowed null for events not anchored to a document.

pipelineRunId.description: Pipeline run id, when applicable. Aligns with `nodeRunId` for cross-engine correlation per architecture §6.11.

nodeRunId.description: Pipeline node run id, when applicable.

traceparent.description: W3C trace context (CSV #20). Correlates this audit event to the originating distributed trace span.

actor.description: Who or what performed the action.

type.description: `SYSTEM` for service-to-service workflow steps; `USER` for human actions (overrides, approvals); `CONNECTOR` for external sources (Drive, Gmail) acting on the user's behalf.

version.description: Service version (semver) so old events identify exactly which build emitted them.

instance.description: Pod / container instance id, for traceability under horizontal scaling.

resource.description: The thing the action acted on. Determines the per-resource hash chain (CSV #4) for Tier 1 events.

version.description: Monotonic version of the resource at the time of the event.

details.description: Event-type-specific payload, partitioned per CSV #6 into `metadata` (always retained) and `content` (subject to right-to-erasure). Tier 1 envelopes store hashes of `content`; Tier 2 envelopes store raw values. The relay is responsible for applying the hash transformation when writing to Tier 1.

metadata.description: Always retained — survives right-to-erasure. Compliance-essential fields: identifiers, timestamps, model versions, confidence scores, tier-of-decision, latency, retention class, etc. No raw user content here.

content.description: Subject to right-to-erasure. At Tier 2: raw values (text snippets, classification rationale, extracted PII). At Tier 1: same keys, but values MUST be `sha256:<hex>` hashes — the relay strips raw values when promoting to Tier 1.

supersedes.description: Per CSV #7: when this event supersedes a prior decision (e.g. reclassification), holds the `eventId` of the prior event. Walking the chain reconstructs the as-of-date view.

supersededBy.description: Per CSV #7: populated retroactively (in a separate forward-only event) when a later event supersedes this one. Auditors traverse `supersededBy` forwards or `supersedes` backwards.

retentionClass.description: Retention bucket for this event. `7Y` for compliance / Tier 1; `90D` / `30D` for Tier 2 with ILM moving cold.

previousEventHash.description: Per CSV #4: per-resource hash chain integrity. The hash of the immediately preceding event in this resource's chain. `null` (or omitted) for the first event in a resource's chain. Tier 2 events do not chain — this field is meaningful only when `tier=DOMAIN`.

allOf.$comment: Tier 1 (DOMAIN) events MUST carry the per-resource hash chain link. First-in-chain events satisfy this with `previousEventHash: null`.
